LAW AND MOTION 302, GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS THE FOLLOWING TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. ON MAY 11, 2023, THE COURT ORDERED THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER. THE MAY 17, 2023, VALENCIA DECLARATION STATES THAT PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL HAS BEEN IGNORING DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL ATTEMPTS TO MEET AND CONFER. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH CCP 430.41. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY JULY 21, 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B, ULMER JR., CLERK: R. WOODS, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

DECLARATION OF SIYUN YANG IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLCS MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM PLAINTIFFS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100198501) FILED BY PLAINTIFF PAULO, JOSEPH B. AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 435.5, WHICH REQUIRES THAT THE PARTIES CONDUCT A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE A MOTION TO STRIKE IS FILED. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY JUNE 12, 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-21-2023 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Off Calendar Oct-10-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar. Plaintiff's request to stay this case pending the California Supreme Court's resolution of Rodriguez v. FCA US, LLC (2022) 77 Cal.App.5th 209 is granted. (CCP 128(a)(8).) The main issue in this case is whether the express warranty provisions of the Song-Beverly Act apply to used vehicles. There is a split of authority regarding that issue and the California Supreme Court recently heard argument on the matter. This court anticipates that the split between Rodriguez and Stiles v. Kia Motors America, Inc. (2024) 101 Cal.App.5th 913, which is also under review, will be resolved shortly. Defendant may seek to lift the stay and calendar an updated summary judgment motion after the California Supreme Court issues its decision.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

Defendant GENERAL MOTORS LLC's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-11-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT

No appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT as follows:

General Motors LLC's motion to strike is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 435.5, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a motion to strike is filed. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant's response to the amended complaint is now due by June 12, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)
